name: Credit Union Of Vermont
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Vermont residents, workers, and families with competitive rates, online banking, and local service since 1958.
description_long:
Credit Union of Vermont was originally chartered in 1958 to serve U.S. Postal Service employees and their families. Over decades, membership expanded to include all Vermonters who valued local, community-focused banking. In January 2026, the organization merged with Vermont Federal Credit Union (founded 1953) to strengthen financial stability and expand service offerings while maintaining its local presence and mission as a member-owned cooperative.

The credit union offers a full range of consumer financial products including savings accounts, money market accounts, term certificates (CDs), vehicle loans, VISA credit cards, home equity lines of credit, online banking, and ATM access. They provide retirement accounts, financial calculators, and direct deposit services. As a division of Vermont Federal Credit Union, members now have access to a broader product and service portfolio while maintaining local branch service in Rutland.

Credit Union of Vermont distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit, member-owned cooperative structure where earnings return to members rather than outside shareholders. The organization maintains strong community ties, offering scholarships named after longtime board members and serving the Vermont community exclusively. Their merger with Vermont Federal Credit Union provides enhanced stability and expanded resources without compromising local service commitment.

The main limitation is geographic eligibility—membership is restricted to individuals who live, work, worship, or attend school in Vermont, plus their family members. As a smaller regional institution post-merger, product offerings and rates may be more limited compared to large national banks, though competitive rates (Money Market at 2.75% APY, Term Certificates at 3.50% APY, vehicle loans from 5.05% APR) are published. The organization's recent merger transition may affect service consistency during 2026.
services: Money Market savings accounts up to 2.75% APY
Vehicle loans (36-month at 5.05% APR, 60-month at 5.50% APR)
Online banking with User ID/password login
24/7 ATM access and night deposit services
pros: Not-for-profit cooperative structure returns earnings to members as competitive rates and lower fees rather than shareholder profits
Money Market accounts offer up to 2.75% APY and Term Certificates up to 3.50% APY, competitive with regional standards
Vehicle loans available at 5.05% APR (36-month) and 5.50% APR (60-month) with transparent rate disclosure
24/7 ATM access through Private Branch network and dedicated night deposit service outside business hours
Online banking and bank-by-phone service (877-596-8728) for remote account management
Member scholarship program ($1,000 awards) demonstrating community investment commitment
NCUA insurance protection for member deposits as federally chartered credit union
cons: Membership restricted to Vermont residents, workers, students, and their families only—no out-of-state eligibility
Limited branch presence with single physical location in Rutland, Vermont reducing accessibility for rural members
Recently merged entity (January 2026) may experience service transition issues and operational inconsistencies
Lobby hours newly shortened to 8:30 AM–5:00 PM Monday-Friday as of April 2026, eliminating early morning walk-up window
No mention of mobile app availability or advanced digital banking features beyond online login and phone banking
best_for: Vermont residents and workers seeking membership-based banking with community accountability
Savers prioritizing competitive CD rates (3.50% APY) and money market returns over convenience banking
Auto buyers in Vermont looking for transparent, published vehicle loan rates without national bank overhead
Individuals valuing not-for-profit financial institutions that return earnings to members instead of shareholders
diagnosis: Credit Union of Vermont is best for Vermont residents seeking community-focused, member-owned banking with competitive savings and loan rates. The primary caveat is strict geographic eligibility (Vermont only) and limited branch access in Rutland, making this unsuitable for out-of-state members or those requiring multi-location convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Credit Union of Vermont (Rutland, VT) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Credit Union of Vermont — NCUA-insured credit union in Rutland, VT. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
